personality_voice.book_baseline: Bletchley appears only in a single narrative reference in the books: he is identified as the Slytherin Keeper who jinxed Gryffindor player Alicia Spinnet from behind in the library, an act of cowardly, unsportsmanlike aggression. No dialogue, internal state, or further behavioral pattern is established beyond this one action.
personality_voice.film_portrayal: Bletchley does not appear in the film corpus at all. No dialogue, action, or reference to him is present.
personality_voice.difference: The character is entirely absent from the films. There is no film material to evaluate.
personality_voice.penalty_logic: A score of 0 is assigned not as a faithfulness judgment but because there is no film portrayal to compare against. The scoring framework cannot measure fidelity when one side of the comparison is empty.

narrative_role_agency.book_baseline: Bletchley's sole narrative function in the books is as an example of Slytherin foul play tolerated by Snape. He acts as a minor antagonist whose off-pitch aggression illustrates the partisan environment surrounding the Gryffindor-Slytherin Quidditch rivalry.
narrative_role_agency.difference: His narrative function is completely absent. No equivalent scene or reference replaces it.
narrative_role_agency.penalty_logic: No film portrayal exists, so no score can be assigned.

motivations_internal_conflict.book_baseline: No motivations beyond implied house rivalry and willingness to cheat are established in the books.

character_arc.book_baseline: No arc exists in the books. Bletchley is a flat, incidental character with a single referenced action.

key_relationships.book_baseline: No meaningful relationship is established in the books beyond his implicit role as a Slytherin player protected by Snape's bias.

complexity_nuance_lost_material.book_baseline: Bletchley has minimal complexity in the books - a single act of cowardly aggression defines him entirely.

lost_or_transferred_material: The jinxing of Alicia Spinnet in the library, attributed to Bletchley in the books, has no equivalent scene or reference in the films.
comparative.key_observations: Miles Bletchley does not appear in the film corpus at all. He is a deeply minor character in the books - defined by a single referenced act of foul play - and is simply absent from the films. All dimension scores are 0 not because the portrayal is unfaithful, but because there is no film portrayal to evaluate. Confidence is Low across all dimensions. This score of 0 should be interpreted as 'unevaluable' rather than 'maximally unfaithful.'
